name: My SEO agency says they “already handle” voice search—how do I know if they’re actually doing it right?
description: Ask your SEO agency to show specific work on question‑based content, featured snippets, FAQ/LocalBusiness schema, mobile speed, and local “near me” queries. If

mainEntity.name: My SEO agency says they “already handle” voice search—how do I know if they’re actually doing it right?

acceptedAnswer.text:
If your current SEO agency hasn’t specifically addressed voice search—question‑based content, schema, and local optimization—there’s a good chance you’re missing opportunities. Traditional SEO work alone rarely covers the nuances of voice.

You don’t need a separate provider, but you do need to confirm that voice considerations are explicitly in the scope and reporting. Ask directly rather than assuming.

Key factors:
- Whether they’re optimizing for featured snippets and question queries
- Use of FAQ and LocalBusiness schema, not just generic metadata
- Attention to mobile speed and Core Web Vitals
- Monitoring of assistant‑friendly queries and “near me” performance

Practically, request a review focused on voice readiness and a plan for closing gaps. If your agency can’t articulate a voice strategy, consider augmenting with specialists or changing vendors.

Putting voice search on the agenda ensures you’re not stuck with 2015‑era SEO in a 2026 search ecosystem.
acceptedAnswer.description: Ask your SEO agency to show specific work on question‑based content, featured snippets, FAQ/LocalBusiness schema, mobile speed, and local “near me” queries. If they can’t demonstrate concrete voice‑focused changes and results, voice search likely isn’t truly covered and you may need added expertise.
